Petition Of Successor Trustee For Approval Of Accounting Of Funds Advanced To The Trust And Trust Beneficiary And For Approval Of Lien Against Real Property For Fees And Expenses Advanced, Petition To Terminate Trust And For Approval Of Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Jul-19-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gregory O'Keefe for Henry Ong. Shirley Gee (Propria Persona).

Mr. O'Keefe stated that the Trustee is functioning in name only, the amount that his client advanced was $35,060.00, and that the request to terminate Trust was withdrawn. Ms. Gee reported that everything is going fine. The Petition of Successor Trustee for Approval of Accounting of finds advanced to the Trust, etc. was granted (subject to review and receipt of order). Mr. O'Keefe to prepare a form of order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee, Without Bond, And To Remove The Trust From Court Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Mar-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gregory O'Keeffe appearing for Counsel Raymond H. Levy for Henry Ong.

The Court will permit resignation of Andrea Leung, appoint Henry Ong with limited authority without authority to sell the property with minimum bond in the amount of $10,000.00 keeping the Trust under court supervision. Ms. Leung is to do a final accounting. Mr. O'Keeffe was told that he may have to file an ex parte petition to vacate the order selling the property. The matter was taken under submission for Mr. O'Keeffe to prepare the order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee, Without Bond, And To Remove The Trust From Court Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Feb-16-2011 Continued To Mar-15-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Andrea Leung (current Trustee). Gregory O'Keeffe appearing for Attorney Raymond Levy for Henry Ong.

Mr. O'Keeffe stated that Katrina does not communicate with her Mother, that she does not understand why she has to sign anything and that she does not want to sign anything, and that he would attempt to indicate to Katrina's Husband that it would be helpful for Katrina to sign a waiver of bond. Ms. Alavi stated that her Client was willing to resign, that they would leave to the Court to determine to take the Trust out of Court supervision, that Shirley Gee was very uncooperative, and that they have not made any progress on selling the house. Mr. O'Keeffe also stated that Shirley Gee was paying the mortgage, that Mr. Ong was prepared to pay the taxes and that there were funds in Mr. Levy's trust account to pay Mr. Siracusa's/Ms. Leung's fees. The matter was continued to March 15, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee, Without Bond, And To Remove The Trust From Court Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Feb-02-2011 Continued To Feb-16-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To List And Sell Real Property And To Enter Into An Exclusive Listing Agreement With Real Estate Broker; Petition To Modify Terms Of Trust Due To Changed Circumstances
Off Calendar Sep-03-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and with Andrea Leung. Shirley Lee Gee in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that there was no order regarding the 2007 hearing granting the previous Trustee authorization to sell the property. Mr. Siracusa explained that no order was submitted because Ms. Adler (previous Trustee) resigned when Ms. Leung was appointed, stated that there was less than $1,000.00 in cash, that they had no control over the rents, that there was deferred maintenance on the property, and that there was a risk of foreclosure and risk of the law suits by tenants. Ms. Gee expressed her concerns, stated that she needed help handling her affairs, that she needed time to obtain a Lawyer, that Ms. Leung was not looking out for her best interest and that she had three rooms available for rent. The Court expressed concern about foreclosure in the near future, delay of sale of the property, the risk of the Trust being embroiled in litigation with Tenants and agreed to read the letters submitted by Ms. Gee from her Doctor. The matters were taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Authorization To Sell Real Property And For Modification Of Terms Of Trust
MINI MINUTES: John Mangini appearing for and with trustee Elizabeth Adler. Mr. LeRoux is not in Court at 9:13am. Case is passed.
At 9:17am, John Mangini appearing for and with trustee Elizabeth Adler. Thomas R. LdRoux appearing for Shirley Gee. Mr. Mangini reported that no money was received from the last hearing. If the property is not sold, it will be lost to foreclosure. The trustee is making the mortgage payments. Mr. LsRoux did not have the answer as to why no rents were turn over to the trustee. The Court has no choice but to grant the petition. Mr. Mangini will prepare the order and send it to Mr. LeRoux for approval as to form. The order is to included description of the proceedings.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: R. Drocco.
